title: Haddock Bake

html:
HADDOCK BAKE

INGREDIENTS

600 g haddock
• 400 milk
• 6 peppercorns
• 2 bay leaves
• 1 onion, sliced into rings
• 650 g potatoes, sliced into thick rounds
• 500 g baby spinach
• 1 packet instant cheese sauce powder
• 1 packet store-bought cheese sauce (Spur's is very nice)
• 125 ml grated Emmental cheese
• 125 ml grated cheddar cheese
• Pinch of nutmeg

METHOD
1. Cook the haddock with the onions, bay leaves, peppercorns and milk in a pot on the stove for 10 minutes.
2. Drain the milk into a jug and flake the fish.  Get rid of the skin and any bones but keep the milk.
3. Cook the potato slices in hot water until just soft
4. Cook the spinach quickly.  Drain well and place in a single layer in a large, greased  oven dish.
5. Heat the milk and whisk in the instant cheese powder. Once it thickens, add the store-bought cheese sauce, the grated cheeses and the pinch of nutmeg. Stir until the cheeses have melted and remove from the heat.
6. Now layer the fish and then the potatoes on top of the spinach in the dish.
7. Pour your cheese sauce over the potatoes.
8. Bake for 20 minutes at 200C
